title: Chamaesyce ammannioides
taxonomy: Chamaesyce ammannioides is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It belongs to the order Malpighiales and the family Euphorbiaceae. The genus Chamaesyce is a part of the tribe Euphorbieae. Chamaesyce ammannioides is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The species is also known by its common name, Amanville spurge.
uses: Chamaesyce ammannioides, also known as seaside spurge, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
